question: Spread: Iwaki FC (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming J2 100 Year Vision League game, scheduled for March 28 at 1:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Iwaki FC" if Iwaki FC win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "AC Nagano Parceiro".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on jleague.jp. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Iwaki FC vs. AC Nagano Parceiro - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the J2 100 Year Vision League game, scheduled for March 28 at 1:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: AC Nagano Parceiro's superior recent form anchors trader consensus favoring them in the Iwaki FC matchup, with the visitors unbeaten in their last five J3 League games while Iwaki has managed just one win amid a four-match skid. Nagano sits comfortably mid-table, six points clear of relegation-threatened Iwaki, who host at Hoyo Stadium but struggle at home (2W-3L-3D lately). No major injuries reported from official updates, though Iwaki's key midfielder Yamashita remains doubtful per beat reports. Head-to-head favors Nagano (2-1-0 last three), and their rest edge after midweek bye could prove decisive against Iwaki's fatigue from consecutive road trips. Markets reflect ~55% implied probability for Nagano win, per crowd wisdom.

description:

events.title: Ventforet Kōfu vs. RB Ōmiya Ardija - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the J2 100 Year Vision League game, scheduled for March 28 at 1:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ventforet Kōfu's robust home record at JIT Recycle Ink Stadium anchors trader sentiment in this J2 League matchup against RB Ōmiya Ardija, with markets implying around 55-60% probability for a Kōfu victory or draw-no-bet edge. Kōfu enter on a three-game unbeaten run, bolstered by midfielder Yamashita's recent scoring form, while Ōmiya Ardija languish after three straight losses and a porous defense conceding 2.2 goals per away match lately. Official injury reports confirm no new absences for Kōfu, but Ōmiya misses key forward Koizumi (hamstring). Head-to-head favors Kōfu (4 wins in last 5), though Ōmiya's counterattacking threat and mid-table promotion stakes add upset potential amid congested fixtures.

Kōfu enjoys home advantage at JIT Recycle Stadium, unbeaten in five prior home matches against bottom-half rivals, plus full squad depth post-rest. Iwaki's fatigue from midweek cup tie contrasts Kōfu's momentum, with standings pressure heightening stakes for promotion-chasing Kōfu. Weather forecast favors dry conditions suiting Kōfu's possession style.

question: US/Israel strike on Fordow nuclear facility by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the U.S. or Israel carries out a kinetic military strike against the Fordow Fuel Enrichment Plant in Iran between the time of market creation and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This includes, but is not limited to, drone and missile strikes, aerial bombings, and kinetic actions carried out by U.S. or Israeli operatives on the ground. Cyber attacks, sanctions, or diplomatic actions will not count toward the resolution of this market.

U.S. or Israeli air, missile, or drone strikes that are intercepted, shot down, or miss their target will not be considered for this market.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Escalating tensions between Israel and Iran drive trader consensus on low odds for a US/Israel strike on Iran's fortified Fordow nuclear facility, following Israel's October 26 precision strikes on Iranian military sites that deliberately spared nuclear and oil infrastructure to avoid broader escalation. Iran's uranium enrichment at Fordow to near-weapons-grade levels—now exceeding 400 kg of 60% purity per IAEA reports—has heightened IAEA scrutiny and Israeli warnings of preemptive action, yet US officials under Biden emphasize diplomacy amid Gaza and Lebanon conflicts. Upcoming US presidential election on November 5 and IAEA board meeting in Vienna could shift dynamics, with a potential Trump win implying more aggressive posture, though mutual deterrence keeps strike probabilities muted.

question: Will Iran strike Dimona by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Iran carries out kinetic military strike on the listed facility between market creation and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This includes, but is not limited to drone and missile strikes, aerial bombings. Cyber attacks, sanctions, or diplomatic actions will not count towards the resolution of this market.

For the purposes of this market, only strikes that cause physical damage to the infrastructure of the facility, will be considered. Missile and drone strikes which are shot down or miss their targets, or make contact in open spaces or around the facility will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

Only military actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will count toward the resolution of this market. Attacks by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count toward the resolution of this market.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

Note: Cyber attacks will not qualify, even if they have kinetic effects.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket leans heavily against a major Iranian strike by March 31, driven by Tehran's post-October restraint in the Israel-Iran shadow war. Following Iran's limited missile barrage on Israel on October 1 and Israel's precise strikes on Iranian military radar sites on October 26—avoiding oil or nuclear facilities—both sides have signaled de-escalation to prevent wider conflict. Proxy pressures persist, with Hezbollah leadership decimated and Houthi ship attacks ongoing, but Tehran faces U.S. sanctions, economic woes, and internal unrest curbing direct action. Upcoming Trump inauguration on January 20 and potential nuclear diplomacy add uncertainty, tilting odds toward status quo amid deterrence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects skepticism of major U.S./Israel strikes deep inside Iran by March 31, driven by U.S. diplomatic restraint under the Biden administration and incoming Trump transition uncertainties. Recent Israeli airstrikes in October 2024 targeted Iranian missile production sites near Tehran, signaling calibrated responses to Tehran's proxy attacks via Hezbollah and Houthis, but avoided nuclear or oil infrastructure to prevent escalation. question: Will UAE strike Iran by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed country initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Iranian soil or any official Iranian embassy or consulate by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones, or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by the listed country's military forces that impact Iranian ground territory or any official Iranian embassy or consulate (e.g., if a weapons depot on Iranian soil is hit by a missile launched by the listed country, this market will resolve to "Yes").

Missiles or drones which are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether they land on Iranian territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by ground operatives of the listed country will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Which countries will conduct military action against Iran by March 31?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed country initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Iranian soil or any official Iranian embassy or consulate by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones, or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by the listed country's military forces that impact Iranian ground territory or any official Iranian embassy or consulate (e.g., if a weapons depot on Iranian soil is hit by a missile launched by the listed country, this market will resolve to "Yes").

Missiles or drones which are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether they land on Iranian territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by ground operatives of the listed country will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects low implied probabilities for major countries launching direct military action against Iran by March 31, driven by sustained restraint despite heightened Middle East tensions. Israel conducted limited strikes on Iranian targets in October 2024 but has since focused on proxies like Hezbollah in Lebanon and Hamas in Gaza, with no new announcements of Iran-focused operations. The US continues Houthi strikes in Yemen— Iran-backed but not mainland Iran—while emphasizing diplomacy on Tehran's nuclear program amid IAEA reports of non-compliance. Incoming Trump administration signals tougher Iran policy post-January 20, but absent concrete triggers like proxy escalations, markets price stability; watch for UN Security Council sessions or Israeli security cabinet decisions.

question: Will another country strike Iran by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any country other than Israel or the United States initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Iranian soil or any official Iranian embassy or consulate by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying “strike” is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones, or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by a country other than Israel or the United States’ military forces that impact Iranian ground territory or any official Iranian embassy or consulate (e.g., if a weapons depot on Iranian soil is hit by a missile or drone launched by such a country, this market will resolve to “Yes”).

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a “Yes” resolution regardless of whether they land on Iranian territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by ground forces of countries other than Israel or the United States will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Israel's vowed retaliation following Iran's October 1 ballistic missile barrage drives trader consensus on the odds of another country striking Iranian territory, with markets pricing in elevated risks of Israeli airstrikes on nuclear or military sites. Netanyahu's statements emphasize consequences, while U.S. officials provide defensive support but counsel restraint to avert regional war, amid ongoing Hezbollah clashes and Houthi threats. Recent Israeli proxy strikes signal escalation potential, though direct hits on Iran remain unconfirmed since April's limited exchange. Traders weigh U.S. election dynamics and Iranian proxy activities; key upcoming catalysts include Israeli security cabinet decisions and any Tehran's preemptive moves, underscoring high uncertainty in cross-border strike probabilities.

question: Will Reza Pahlavi lead Iran in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Reza Pahlavi de facto holds and exercises the powers of the head of state of Iran by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Reza Pahlavi will be considered to hold power if he exercises primary governing authority over the Iranian state during the specified timeframe, including effective control over the armed forces, national institutions, and core executive decision-making, regardless of formal title, constitutional designation, or international recognition.

Formal appointment, recognition by the United Nations, or recognition by foreign governments is not required.

Indicators of de facto authority may include control over the armed forces and security services; control over executive ministries and state administrative institutions; enforcement of national laws; issuance of binding national directives; and effective control over the capital and core state infrastructure.

Symbolic status, foreign recognition without domestic control, exile leadership without effective authority inside Iran, nomination without effective authority, or ceremonial or transitional status without governing control will not qualify.

If Iran experiences a period in which no individual exercises effective governing control, this will not alone qualify for a “Yes” resolution unless Reza Pahlavi subsequently meets the criteria above within the specified timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Will Reza Pahlavi lead Iran in 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Reza Pahlavi de facto holds and exercises the powers of the head of state of Iran by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Reza Pahlavi will be considered to hold power if he exercises primary governing authority over the Iranian state during the specified timeframe, including effective control over the armed forces, national institutions, and core executive decision-making, regardless of formal title, constitutional designation, or international recognition.

eventMetadata.context_description: Iran's Islamic Republic maintains firm control under Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps, driving trader consensus to price Reza Pahlavi leading the country in 2026 at just 15.2% ("No" at 84.8%). Recent parliamentary elections delivered overwhelming hardliner victories amid historically low voter turnout of under 25%, underscoring regime resilience despite persistent economic sanctions, inflation, and sporadic protests. Pahlavi, operating from exile in the United States, has amplified calls for secular democracy and met with Israeli leaders, but lacks a viable domestic opposition network or military backing inside Iran. Heightened Israel-Iran missile exchanges in 2024 have not precipitated collapse, with security forces effectively suppressing dissent. No scheduled transitions or uprisings signal change by 2026.
